title: Achudemia boniana
intro: Achudemia boniana, also known as Chinese lotus tree or Himalayan lotus tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Saxifragaceae, native to China and the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other diseases. The bioactive constituents of Achudemia boniana include alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being made into a dried powder or capsule for oral consumption.
taxonomy: Achudemia boniana is classified within the family Staphylocactaceae. It belongs to the genus Achudemia and the order Solanales.
